description:
Two point distribution maps, (a) and (b), displaying ground motion data for the Neuchâtel Lake region in Switzerland, overlaid on a light base map showing roads and settlements.
Both maps use a continuous colour scale representing vertical displacement in millimetres [mm]. The scale ranges from -9 mm (dark red) through 0 mm (green) to 9 mm (dark blue). Red colours indicate subsidence (negative displacement), blue colours indicate uplift (positive displacement), and green indicates little to no motion.
Map (a) shows numerous data points with a visible presence of both dark red (subsidence) and dark blue (uplift) values, distributed across the region and often aligning with linear infrastructure or geological features.
Map (b) shows a similar distribution of data points but with a generally higher prevalence of green points and fewer pronounced dark red or dark blue points compared to map (a), suggesting less extreme ground motion in the depicted scenario or dataset. Specific reference years or data sources are not visible.
